title: Lighting Models (PBR/Phong)
description: Understand Phong specular reflection models, microfacet theory, Cook-Torrance BRDF, and Physically Based Rendering (PBR).
learningObjectives: Differentiate empirical models (Phong) from physical models (PBR)
Explain specular, diffuse, and ambient reflection components
Implement light calculations using vectors: Normal (N), Light (L), View (V)

sections.title: Empirical vs Physically Based Lighting
sections.content:
Lighting models calculate pixel color values based on surface vectors:

1. **Phong Model (Empirical)**: Approximates lighting using three components: Ambient (background bounce), Diffuse (Lambertian scattering), and Specular (shiny highlight based on reflection vector).
2. **PBR Model (Physical)**: Uses the Cook-Torrance Bidirectional Reflective Distribution Function (BRDF). Simulates light interaction using physical material parameters (roughness, metalness) and conserves energy (diffuse reflection + specular reflection ≤ incoming light).

sections.title: Phong Specular Light Equation
